title: Notopleura aligera
taxonomy: Notopleura aligera is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Notopleura, which belongs to the family Rubiaceae. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. Notopleura aligera is a plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Notopleura aligera is further categorized into its species, genus, and family levels, providing a detailed understanding of its botanical identity.

intro: Notopleura aligera, a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, is a medicinal plant traditionally used in the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Notopleura aligera include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as notopleurine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
